entries.claim: Cloud restore is refused when the opt-in flag is off (default).
Local-file restore recovers the exact archived bytes with no token and no network.
A crash after the Graph POST but before the local record does not duplicate the item; recovery reconciles to exactly one.
entries.evidence: C4 crash after POST, before record       Reconciled       1        1  OK  ||  ADR-001 invariant holds: exactly one cloud item in every case, no duplicates.

entries.claim: A concurrent identical restore is rejected by the ledger's unique idempotency key, so only one item is created.
The mass-delete guard halts an over-threshold deletion in both directions.
An interrupted chunked upload resumes from persisted session state across a process kill.
The content-derived idempotency key is deterministic and per-field sensitive.
Mail restore is wired through the ledger: a crash after the POST landed reconciles without creating a duplicate (proven against a non-idempotent fake mailbox).
entries.notes: Live marker-probe confirmation is recorded in EV-010; cloud_restore_enabled stays false by default as a deliberate destructive-operation opt-in.

entries.claim: The daemon recovers an in-flight mail restore on boot, reconciling without a new create.
Live: Microsoft Graph preserves a posted Message-ID as internetMessageId and an internetMessageId $filter finds it — the marker probe the crash recovery relies on.
entries.evidence: POST /me/messages -> 201; internetMessageId == posted Message-ID; GET $filter -> 1 match; cleanup DELETE -> 204; VERDICT YES

entries.claim: A not-yet-ledger-backed cloud restore (todo/onenote) is refused before any store access or token lookup, never run via a non-crash-safe direct POST.
Restore OAuth scopes request delegated Notes.ReadWrite and never the admin-only Notes.ReadWrite.All scope.
The local API defaults to an owner-only Unix socket on Unix, while opt-in TCP rejects non-loopback Host and non-local Origin before routing.
entries.command: cargo test -p isyncyou-cli parses_serve_defaults_to_unix_socket_transport && cargo test -p isyncyou-cli parses_serve_tcp_opt_in && cargo test -p isyncyou-daemon parses_defaults && cargo test -p isyncyou-webui tcp_rejects_non_loopback_host_and_origin
entries.evidence: test tests::parses_serve_defaults_to_unix_socket_transport ... ok; test tests::parses_serve_tcp_opt_in ... ok; test tests::parses_defaults ... ok; test serve::tests::tcp_rejects_non_loopback_host_and_origin ... ok

entries.claim: The standalone doctor hard-fails missing config, treats missing local store/auth cache as warnings, reports plaintext token caches unless protection is configured, and accepts keyring marker caches.
entries.evidence: test tests::missing_config_is_fail ... ok; test tests::healthy_config_with_missing_store_is_warn_not_fail ... ok; test tests::missing_login_token_is_an_auth_warning ... ok; test tests::plaintext_login_token_is_an_auth_warning_without_secret ... ok; test tests::encrypted_login_token_with_secret_is_ok ... ok; test tests::keyring_login_token_marker_is_ok_without_plaintext_secret ... ok

entries.claim: Path mapping dedupes Unicode-composed and Unicode-decomposed sibling names instead of creating an ambiguous cloud collision.
Shared IPC transfer/status payloads serialize with canonical service/status wire names and round-trip through JSON.
TokenCache::save tightens a Unix token-cache file to owner-only mode while preserving loadability.
The sanitized mail viewer embeds matching safe cid images as data URLs and strips unresolved cid/remote resources.
entries.command: cargo test -p isyncyou-webui mail_page_maps_cid_images_to_data_urls && cargo test -p isyncyou-webui sanitize_strips_scripts_and_blocks_remote_resources && cargo test -p isyncyou-connectors extract_html_with_inline_images_collects_safe_cid_parts
entries.evidence: test view::tests::mail_page_maps_cid_images_to_data_urls ... ok; test view::tests::sanitize_strips_scripts_and_blocks_remote_resources ... ok; test mime::tests::extract_html_with_inline_images_collects_safe_cid_parts ... ok

entries.claim: Archived-mail external http(s) links are rewritten to a local confirmation page, unsafe schemes are rejected, and the dialog is CSP-locked.
entries.command: cargo test -p isyncyou-webui mail_page_rewrites_external_links_to_confirm_dialog && cargo test -p isyncyou-webui external_link_dialog_page_requires_safe_http_url && cargo test -p isyncyou-webui open_external_confirms_only_safe_http_urls
entries.evidence: test view::tests::mail_page_rewrites_external_links_to_confirm_dialog ... ok; test view::tests::external_link_dialog_page_requires_safe_http_url ... ok; test tests::open_external_confirms_only_safe_http_urls ... ok

entries.claim: Personal-account contact restore keeps writable contact fields but strips photo objects and photo sidecar metadata instead of attempting an unsupported contact-photo upload.
OneNote page resource URLs from archived HTML are allowlisted, fetched to on-disk resource files, and mapped by a per-page manifest while non-OneNote remote URLs are ignored.
Capability-token-authorized restore POSTs write durable started/result audit entries without logging the capability token.
entries.command: cargo test -p isyncyou-webui restore_post_writes_a_durable_audit_log && cargo test -p isyncyou-webui restore_post_audits_handler_errors

entries.claim: OneNote resource-bearing restore uses a multipart Presentation plus binary data-part payload, while plain pages keep the text/html path.
entries.command: cargo test -p isyncyou-connectors restore_page_with_resources_uses_multipart_parts && cargo test -p isyncyou-connectors restore_page_uses_plain_html_when_no_resources && cargo test -p isyncyou-graph --features http onenote_multipart_body_uses_presentation_and_binary_parts && cargo test -p isyncyou-graph --features http onenote_multipart_body_rejects_header_injection
entries.evidence: test restore::tests::restore_page_with_resources_uses_multipart_parts ... ok; test restore::tests::restore_page_uses_plain_html_when_no_resources ... ok; test http::tests::onenote_multipart_body_uses_presentation_and_binary_parts ... ok; test http::tests::onenote_multipart_body_rejects_header_injection ... ok

entries.claim: Restore and scheduled-sync web UI POSTs use separate injected capability tokens, and cross-token attempts are rejected.
entries.command: cargo test -p isyncyou-webui destructive_capability_tokens_are_action_scoped && cargo test -p isyncyou-webui index_injects_separate_capability_tokens_when_enabled

entries.claim: The TCP local API refuses wildcard, LAN, and arbitrary-host bind addresses before opening a listener, while preserving loopback binds.
entries.command: cargo test -p isyncyou-webui tcp_bind_address_must_be_loopback && cargo test -p isyncyou-webui serve_refuses_non_loopback_bind_address

entries.claim: Credential-backed token-cache encryption writes an authenticated envelope that hides token strings, round-trips with the right secret, and rejects the wrong secret.
entries.command: cargo test -p isyncyou-graph token_cache_encrypted_roundtrip_hides_plaintext && cargo test -p isyncyou-graph token_cache_encrypted_roundtrip_rejects_wrong_secret && cargo test -p isyncyou-graph token_cache_secret_file_is_trimmed_and_nonempty
entries.evidence: test auth::tests::token_cache_encrypted_roundtrip_hides_plaintext ... ok; test auth::tests::token_cache_encrypted_roundtrip_rejects_wrong_secret ... ok; test auth::tests::token_cache_secret_file_is_trimmed_and_nonempty ... ok

entries.claim: Desktop keyring login stores token JSON behind a Secret Service / KDE Wallet compatible keyring entry, leaves only a marker file on disk, and refresh saves preserve the keyring backend.
entries.command: cargo test -p isyncyou-graph --features desktop-keyring token_cache_keyring_roundtrip_writes_marker_only && cargo test -p isyncyou-graph --features desktop-keyring token_cache_save_preserves_existing_keyring_backend && cargo test -p isyncyou-cli parses_login
entries.evidence: test auth::tests::token_cache_keyring_roundtrip_writes_marker_only ... ok; test auth::tests::token_cache_save_preserves_existing_keyring_backend ... ok; test tests::parses_login ... ok

entries.claim: SQLCipher-backed store encryption hides item metadata and body-index text, fails closed without the right key, and keeps VACUUM/PBS snapshots encrypted.
entries.command: cargo test -p isyncyou-store encrypted_store_hides_plaintext_and_requires_key && cargo test -p isyncyou-store encrypted_backup_to_keeps_snapshot_encrypted && cargo test -p isyncyou-store store_secret_file_is_trimmed_and_nonempty
entries.evidence: test tests::encrypted_store_hides_plaintext_and_requires_key ... ok; test tests::encrypted_backup_to_keeps_snapshot_encrypted ... ok; test tests::store_secret_file_is_trimmed_and_nonempty ... ok

entries.claim: The release path can generate a CycloneDX SBOM from the locked Cargo graph and the release workflow uploads/attests the SBOM and release artifacts.
All external GitHub Actions workflow dependencies are pinned to immutable 40-character commit SHAs.
entries.evidence: workflow action pins: 55 action refs checked | 12 workflow file(s); OK - every external workflow action is pinned to a commit SHA.

entries.claim: Live: PBS backup creates a real snapshot, PBS restore imports it into a temporary directory, the restored manifest/store are readable, and the test snapshot is forgotten.
entries.evidence: backup_snapshot=host/isyncyou-primary/2026-06-05T15:46:31Z; restore_manifest_account=primary; restore_manifest_schema_version=4; restore_store_user_version=4; restore_store_table_count=15; cleanup_snapshot_remaining=0; VERDICT PBS ROUNDTRIP YES; temporary token deleted after probe.

entries.claim: Calendar restore is wired through the ledger: the sink stamps the Graph transactionId marker the crash-recovery probe later searches for, and the event sanitizer drops read-only fields before the POST.
entries.notes: Calendar crash-safety is proven against a fake calendar that models Graph's transactionId de-dup, by the crash-matrix in restore_calendar::tests (happy_path / crash_after_post_landed / crash_before_post_landed / boot_recovery_reconciles_a_pending_op_without_duplicating). Recovery converges via the idempotent re-POST; EV-034 is the live confirmation.

entries.claim: engine::restore_cloud routes a calendar restore past the crash-safety gate to the calendar ledger path (it is not refused as unsupported), reaching the account lookup.

Live: Microsoft Graph honours a posted transactionId on POST /me/events and de-duplicates server-side (re-POST returns the same event id) — the idempotent re-create the calendar crash recovery relies on. A transactionId $filter is not supported (HTTP 400), confirming why recovery re-POSTs instead of probing.
entries.evidence: POST /me/events -> 201; transactionId preserved; re-POST same transactionId -> 201 same id: True; GET $filter transactionId -> 400 (supported: False); cleanup DELETE -> 204; VERDICT transactionId server-side de-dup: YES

entries.claim: Contacts restore is wired through the ledger (mail-shape): the sink stamps the single-value extended-property marker the recovery probe later searches for, and sanitize_contact drops read-only/photo fields before the POST.
entries.notes: Crash-safety for contacts is proven against a non-idempotent fake by the crash-matrix in restore_contacts::tests (happy_path / crash_after_post_landed / crash_before_post_landed / boot_recovery_reconciles_a_pending_op_without_creating); EV-037 is the live confirmation.

entries.claim: engine::restore_cloud routes a contacts restore past the crash-safety gate to the contacts ledger path (it is not refused as unsupported), reaching the account lookup.

Live: Microsoft Graph accepts a single-value extended property on POST /me/contacts and a singleValueExtendedProperties $filter finds the contact again — the marker probe the contacts crash recovery relies on.
entries.evidence: POST /me/contacts -> 201; GET $filter singleValueExtendedProperties -> 200, matches: 1; cleanup DELETE -> 204; VERDICT create accepts extended property: YES; $filter finds it: YES
